title: QUIC RFC 9000 NEW_TOKEN ACK Piggyback Topoff Architecture
purpose: Describe the bounded explicit-path 1-RTT NEW_TOKEN packet-composition topoff that reuses the Application Data ACK piggyback policy when a server emits a token after validating a client address.
design_summary: The explicit-path 1-RTT application send helper, currently used by server NEW_TOKEN emission after path validation, performs the same transient ACK-prefix composition used by the active-path stream send helper. When Application Data ACK state is pending, the helper protects ACK plus NEW_TOKEN in one Application Data packet, marks the ACK frame as sent under the carrier packet number, and keeps the original NEW_TOKEN payload as the retransmittable plaintext. If no ACK is pending, the helper emits the NEW_TOKEN payload unchanged.
data_and_state_considerations: The topoff does not add a second ACK scheduler or a path-selection policy. It reuses the existing Application Data ACK-generation state and the existing server-only token-emission table. ACK bytes are transient packet-composition data; the sent-packet retransmission payload remains the original NEW_TOKEN frame bytes.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Only explicit-path 1-RTT NEW_TOKEN packet composition is in scope.
No ACK frame is invented when ACK-generation state has no pending Application Data receipt.
A piggybacked ACK frame is recorded under the actual protected packet number so duplicate ACK-only sends for the same trigger remain suppressed.
Server-only NEW_TOKEN emission and per-address deduplication remain owned by ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0002.
alternatives_considered: Widen ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0018 after the fact; rejected because the active-path stream-send slice was intentionally committed with a narrower boundary.
Treat all candidate-path application sends as covered; rejected because the executable seam proven here is the explicit-path NEW_TOKEN helper only.
Delay this until Initial and Handshake packet-builder piggybacking; rejected because NEW_TOKEN uses the existing 1-RTT Application Data helper and can be proven independently.
risks: Initial and Handshake ACK piggyback policy remains unclaimed.
General candidate-path or multipath scheduling remains unclaimed beyond the server NEW_TOKEN explicit-path topoff.
Endpoint interop success and public API behavior remain outside this architecture slice.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This design covers pending Application Data ACK piggybacking on server NEW_TOKEN packets emitted through the explicit-path 1-RTT application send helper. It does not claim Initial or Handshake ACK piggybacking, a general delayed-ACK timer, endpoint interop success, or broader multipath scheduling.
